title: Aureline setting-definition row
description: Boundary schema for a single setting-definition row in the settings registry. See docs/adr/0008-settings-definition-and-effective-configuration-resolver.md. Every setting lives under one canonical setting_id and declares its value type, allowed scopes, precedence posture, alias_set, migration table, capability dependencies, preview class, restart posture, lifecycle label, redaction class, and docs strings. The eventual settings crate's Rust types are the schema of record; this file is the cross-tool boundary that the UI, CLI, command palette, docs renderer, policy explainer, AI 'Explain why' affordance, profile import/export, optional sync, mutation-journal renderer, and support-bundle exporter read. Adding a new field, type class, scope, lifecycle label, denial reason, preview class, restart posture, control-authority class, or capability dependency kind is additive-minor and bumps settings_schema_version; repurposing an existing value is breaking and requires a new decision row.

settings_schema_version.description: Integer schema version for this settings-vocabulary export. Current value is 1. Bumped only on breaking payload changes; additive-optional fields do not bump this version.

setting_id.description: Canonical stable dotted lower-snake setting id. Stable once the setting leaves 'experimental' lifecycle; may not be repurposed after that point.

scope_id.description: Frozen allowed-scope id. The resolver evaluates the first nine in precedence order; admin_policy_narrowing is orthogonal and caps the effective value rather than replacing it.

lifecycle_label.description: Lifecycle label. Retired settings refuse writes and return the frozen retired-value notice on read.

preview_class.description: Preview/rollback posture enforced uniformly by the resolver at write time.

restart_posture.description: Declared restart posture. A surface that silently requires a restart not declared here is a bug.

redaction_class.description: ADR-0007 redaction class applied when the value reaches an exportable surface (logs, support bundle, evidence packet, profile export, sync).

transform_class.description: Frozen migration transform-class set. type_change_breaking is NOT a transform class; it requires a new setting_id with a redirect alias.

from_id.description: Legacy id that redirects to this canonical setting.

since_version.description: Version in which the alias began redirecting to this id.

deprecated_in_version.description: Version in which the legacy id was marked deprecated; null if still active.

removal_target_version.description: Version in which the legacy id is scheduled to be retired; null if no removal is scheduled.

alias_direction.description: Only direction permitted. Aliases never mint new identities; they redirect onto the canonical setting_id.

alias_row.description: One alias row redirecting a legacy id to this canonical setting_id.

from_shape.description: Prior value-shape descriptor. Opaque at the schema boundary; the eventual settings crate's Rust types own the concrete shape.

to_shape.description: Current value-shape descriptor. Opaque at the schema boundary.

is_lossy.description: True when the migration cannot represent some prior values exactly. Lossy migrations MUST create a rollback checkpoint before apply.

rollback_supported.description: True when the migration can be reversed via the ADR-0006 mutation-journal checkpoint.

divergence_marker.description: Opaque marker the resolver attaches to values that cannot be represented in the current shape; surfaces render a visible divergence notice.

migration_row.description: One per-version migration row describing how a prior value shape translates into the current one.

class.description: Declared value-type class. Surfaces read the shape from this definition row, not from observed values.

min.description: Inclusive lower bound for integer/float classes. NaN forbidden for float.

max.description: Inclusive upper bound for integer/float classes.

regex.description: Optional regex pattern constraining string values.

length_min.description: Optional inclusive lower length bound for string values.

length_max.description: Optional inclusive upper length bound for string values.

enum_values.description: Closed enum set. Widening the enum is additive-minor; narrowing is breaking.

element_type.description: Declared element type for array_of<T>. Opaque at the schema boundary; the concrete element shape is owned by the settings crate's Rust types.

discriminator.description: Discriminator field name for tagged_union values.

object_schema_ref.description: Opaque handle pointing at the settings crate's object-shape row for object/tagged_union values.

credential_secret_class.description: ADR-0007 secret-class that the credential_alias resolves against. Raw secret bytes never appear in settings artifacts.

duration_canonical_unit.description: Canonical monotonic unit the resolver normalises duration values into.

value_type.description: Typed value-shape descriptor. Surfaces read the type class and constraints from this block rather than inferring from observed values.

kind.description: Frozen capability-dependency kind. Adding a kind is additive-minor and bumps settings_schema_version.

flag_id.description: Required feature flag id for feature_flag_required.

trust_state.description: Required minimum trust state for trust_state_minimum (ADR-0001).

policy_epoch_minimum.description: Required minimum policy epoch for policy_epoch_minimum.

workspace_capability.description: Declared capability id from the ADR-0006 root-capability envelope.

extension_capability.description: Declared capability id from the extension-host registry.

credential_secret_class.description: ADR-0007 secret-class whose handle must resolve for credential_handle_class.

capability_dependency.description: One capability-dependency row. The resolver sets lock_state=capability_locked when any dependency is unmet; the effective-setting record's value reflects the last-known-valid value until the dependency resolves.

value_preview.description: Redacted preview of the default value for this scope; never raw secret material. Typed per the declared value_type.

source_label.description: Default source tag rendered by 'show source' affordances when this scope wins.

default_value_row.description: Default value bound to a specific scope (typically built_in_default or channel_or_experiment_default).

allowed_scopes.description: Set of scopes in which this setting MAY be written. The resolver denies writes from scopes outside this set with 'scope_not_allowed_for_setting'. built_in_default is implicitly readable. admin_policy_narrowing, when present, means the setting honours admin-policy narrowing (default for every setting unless an explicit decision row excludes it).

alias_set.description: Ordered list of legacy ids that redirect to this canonical setting. Empty for new settings.

migration_table.description: Per-version migration rows describing how a prior value shape translates into the current one.

capability_dependencies.description: Capability dependencies evaluated on read. If any is unmet, lock_state is capability_locked.

default_values.description: Scope-bound default values. Most commonly a single built_in_default row.

summary.description: One-sentence summary rendered by the UI, CLI, docs, policy explainer, and AI 'Explain why' affordance.

description.description: Longer description. Rendered by the settings UI detail panel and the CLI 'settings describe' command.

help_doc_ref.description: Stable doc handle pointing at a longer help page. Resolved by the docs lane; never a mutable URL.

change_guidance.description: Short guidance shown before apply for preview-class and rollback-class settings (for example, 'Changing this setting widens AI egress; review carefully').

since_version.description: First product version that shipped this setting.

decision_row_ref.description: Reference to the decision row in artifacts/governance/decision_index.yaml that authorised this setting's posture. Required for settings whose preview_class is rollback_checkpoint_and_approval_required or whose allowed_scopes include admin_policy_narrowing-only semantics.

is_machine_specific.description: True when the setting is semantically machine-local and MUST NOT be carried by the optional-sync lane by default.

is_synced_by_default.description: True when the optional-sync lane carries this setting by default. Mutually exclusive with is_machine_specific.

is_policy_narrowable.description: True when admin-policy narrowing may cap this setting's effective value. Default true.

setting_definition_row.description: One setting-definition row. One canonical id per row; aliases redirect, they do not mint parallel rows.
